# Manifest of cmake-xray Console Quiet/Verbose goldens (AP M5-1.5).
#
# Each non-comment, non-blank line names exactly one .txt golden file in
# this directory. Tranche B.1 adds the Quiet goldens; Tranche B.2 will add
# the Verbose goldens (analyze-verbose, impact-verbose plus targets/file-
# api-only/empty variants). tests/validate_verbosity_reports.py checks
# that the manifest and the directory contents stay in lockstep.
#
# Coverage of the B.1 Console-Vertrag (plan ~213-237):
#   Quiet Analyze pflichtzeilen + optional `target metadata` line
#                            -> console-analyze-quiet-targets.txt
#                               (m4/with_targets, --top 2: ranking truncated,
#                                 hotspot list complete, target_metadata=loaded,
#                                 has-warnings)
#   Quiet Analyze with `target metadata` line omitted
#                            -> console-analyze-quiet.txt
#                               (m3/report_project, --top 2, no targets)
#   Quiet Analyze "lean" run (target_metadata omitted, hotspots empty)
#                            -> console-analyze-quiet-empty.txt
#                               (m5/dot-fixtures/multi_tu_compile_db, default
#                                 --top, 0 hotspots; covers the optional
#                                 `target metadata` omission together with the
#                                 zero-hotspot ranking)
#   Quiet Impact pflichtzeilen + heuristic + no targets
#                            -> console-impact-quiet.txt
#                               (m3/report_impact_header + include/common/config.h)
#   Quiet Impact + heuristic + populated targets
#                            -> console-impact-quiet-targets.txt
#                               (m4/with_targets + include/common/shared.h)
#   Quiet Impact "empty" run (zero affected TUs and zero affected targets)
#                            -> console-impact-quiet-empty.txt
#                               (m4/file_api_only + include/common/shared.h)
#
# Optional-line omission for `warnings: present` is covered by the
# adapter unit test (tests/adapters/test_cli_console_renderers.cpp) using
# synthetic Result objects, because include-resolver warnings make a
# warning-free run hard to reproduce from existing fixtures.

# Quiet goldens
console-analyze-quiet.txt
console-analyze-quiet-targets.txt
console-analyze-quiet-empty.txt
console-impact-quiet.txt
console-impact-quiet-targets.txt
console-impact-quiet-empty.txt
